etymology.description: 老(老) (lǎo, "old") refers to someone of age and thus implies respect and experience. 师(師) (shī, "teacher") denotes someone who imparts knowledge or skills. Combining these, 老师(老師) (lǎoshī) suggests a "respected person who teaches" or a "teacher."
etymology.interpretation: The term 老师(老師) (lǎoshī) implies respect through age, commonly associated with wisdom, making it a respectful title for someone who imparts knowledge.

examples.chinese: 老师(老師)今天给我们布置了很多作业(今天給我們布置了很多作業)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī jīntiān gěi wǒmen bùzhìle hěn duō zuòyè.
examples.english: The teacher assigned us a lot of homework today.

examples.chinese: 我们的老师(我們的老師)很有耐心(很有耐心)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen de lǎoshī hěn yǒu nàixīn.

examples.chinese: 老师说(老師說)学习是一个持续的过程(學習是一個持續的過程)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī shuō xuéxí shì yī gè chíxù de guòchéng.
examples.english: The teacher says learning is a continuous process.
